$comment: 118R pre-migration snapshot for summarize-document-for-workspace@v1 — captured 2026-06-25 against spaarkedev1 (DEV) via Dataverse MCP read_query before any node-graph mutation. This is the rollback artifact: re-applying the inverse of section 'forward-migration' below recovers the snapshot state. See notes/handoffs/118R-migration-evidence.md for the rollback recipe.

playbook.$comment-playbookcode: Null today. Phase 1 backfill never reached this row (task 014 in Phase 1 only backfilled six other production-bound playbooks). The vector-match path uses sprk_jpsmatchingmetadata / embeddings — not the slug. NOT required for 118R.

nodes.$comment-sprk_actionid: Links to SUM-CHAT@v1 (sprk_analysisaction id eeb05bfd-1260-f111-ab0b-70a8a59455f4). Full action FK lookup omitted from this snapshot — the canonical record is the action row itself, not this node's FK projection. See referencedAction below.

referencedAction.$comment-outputSchema: These FOUR top-level fields are the sectionName candidates for the migrated composite node. Order is load-bearing per task 006 spike: tldr -> summary -> keywords -> entities (Azure OpenAI Structured Outputs streams in declaration order). 118R preserves this order in the migrated composite's sections[] array.

chatSiblingBaseline.$comment: Chat sibling is NOT migrated by 118R (FR-58 / ADR-037). Baseline captured to demonstrate the pre-migration structural distinction. Chat sibling MUST remain single-action.

schemaGapBlocker.title: Dataverse sprk_nodetype choice column does NOT include DeliverComposite (100000004)
schemaGapBlocker.impact: Until the option-set is extended with value 100000004, ANY POST/PATCH against sprk_playbooknode that sets sprk_nodetype=100000004 will be rejected by Dataverse with a choice-value-not-valid error. The C# engine + executor (DeliverCompositeNodeExecutor, NodeType.DeliverComposite) ARE in place from task 114R; the gap is purely on the Dataverse metadata side.
schemaGapBlocker.remediation: Add the choice option via Dataverse metadata API (PATCH https://{org}.crm.dynamics.com/api/data/v9.2/EntityDefinitions(LogicalName='sprk_playbooknode')/Attributes(LogicalName='sprk_nodetype')) — out of 118R scope; tracked as a follow-up below. This is NOT 'data migration' work; it is a Dataverse schema-metadata change requiring the dataverse-create-schema skill.

forward-migration.$comment: When the schema gap above is resolved, the deployment file infra/dataverse/playbooks/summarize-document-for-workspace-v1-multinode.json is the authoritative target state. Apply via scripts/Deploy-Playbook.ps1 -DefinitionFile <file> -Force (Force is required because the playbook already exists; idempotent skip-by-name would otherwise no-op).

intendedFinalState.playbookRow: UNCHANGED — sprk_analysisplaybookid 302e6da6-... preserved (immutable opaque ID); sprk_name 'summarize-document-for-workspace@v1' preserved (slug unchanged so vector match still routes here).

nodes.name: deliverComposite
nodes.$comment: NEW node type — requires Dataverse choice add (100000004)

intendedFinalState.$comment-action-reuse: All four Action nodes reference the SAME existing SUM-CHAT@v1 action — outputSchema is intrinsic to the action; the playbook just invokes it four times. Per-section prompt focus is achieved via per-node configJson template parameters (styleFrom: 'extract-tldr-only' etc.) — the action's JPS prompt branches on the focus hint. NO new sprk_analysisaction rows are created. This preserves NFR-02 (production-bound playbook preservation): the output shape that StructuredOutputStreamWidget consumes is unchanged.

rollback.$comment: If the post-migration state is rejected or causes regression, restore via:
steps: Re-set the original single node 'summarize' (sprk_playbooknodeid e35cbcd3-026c-f111-ab0e-7ced8ddc4a05) to statecode=0 statuscode=1 via MCP update_record (it should remain Active and untouched anyway — see ADR-037 deactivation pattern below).
Delete the new Action nodes via MCP delete_record (extract-tldr / extract-summary / extract-keywords / extract-entities) — capture their GUIDs from the post-migration evidence note before deleting.
Delete the new DeliverComposite node via MCP delete_record.

Optional: re-run scripts/Deploy-Playbook.ps1 -DefinitionFile src/server/api/Sprk.Bff.Api/Services/Ai/Chat/Playbooks/summarize-document-for-workspace.playbook.json -Force to re-apply the legacy single-node state from the source-of-truth JSON.
